_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to build complex data structures and high-level programming languages.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of Python programming language fundamentals for data applications, including data structures, control flow, object-oriented and functional programming patterns, standard library capabilities, and the data science ecosystem (NumPy, Pandas, scikit-learn).
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-S-001 (TF, cosine: 0.505), TF-A-001 (TF, cosine: 0.486).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.0/10 (raw 0.498). Shared vocabulary: [data, programming, structures]. DCWF-only terms [ability, build, complex, high, languages, level] vs WIDAI-only terms [applications, capabilities, control, ecosystem, flow, functional] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.878 (P=0.906, R=0.852). P exceeds R by 0.054 — the DCWF element's 10 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 30 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Ability to build complex data structures and high-level programming languages." and "Knowledge of Python programming language fundamentals for data applications, inc" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.536 ≈ STS 0.498 (gap 0.037). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [data, programming, structures] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 11.4%. Shared tokens: [data, programming, structures]. DCWF-only: [ability, build, complex, high, languages, level]. WIDAI-only: [applications, capabilities, control, ecosystem, flow, functional].
